textByHash.d461da246fd1ce2cb3c1e7c7cb556ae5046a8db1: That is the job of a spy, Maisa. I remember that, but it seems you do not.
textByHash.efcbd894cab7d53517e957ba3800b815a13a34b0: We had a plan, Maisa. You were ordered to stay in Menaphos. Now your recklessness has cost us dearly.
textByHash.cf60fea2f8ff8b2a08b913b2d67f860bf74455cd: Yes... Kaleef. Did you think I wasn't aware of your little romance?
textByHash.f5adcd8b845057d0888b346b60bb5eb7751273cc: Did you take me for a fool? I've been dealing in deception since before you were born. Yours was not hard to spot.
textByHash.dba22e756277332e2215a04b68905616d14152f1: I made the mistake of letting it slide. A poor move on my part. Kaleef's death has clearly impacted your ability to think rationally.
textByHash.28bd5f477cdc3ac9f14fa4e1bae2fd37b6a7c280: I serve Al Kharid, Maisa, just as you once did.
textByHash.de0fe34cadfc8f5b1f77284876be48f890d021d7: I hope you don't believe yourself innocent in all of this. You have been meddling in affairs that are not your concern.
textByHash.b65fdf6d8fd0bf659644552c2050f6e673dbf170: By curing the pox? Tell me, who does that help? Al Kharid or Menaphos?
textByHash.d838fd89027708040ee170da10d8839795538e8f: On paper perhaps, but our two cities were at war for years. Signing a few treaties does not stop us from being enemies.
textByHash.ac17be066896855528ba29ae6356c249b69e72f8: It will surely lead to the end of the Menaphos quarantine. That is not to our benefit. We wanted that city isolated.
textByHash.0bf84bf77926f386013633d6ba9bd8c298bb13be: The Devourer? You speak of legends and fairy tales.
textByHash.96322dc420543e01b1f991ed5568f015dbaea84b: Maybe once, but that was a long time ago.
textByHash.21b9a818d4f28d7f8acb5d4d46e3afa3d405946d: And these weapons are in Menaphos?
textByHash.c61af2f06f030270eefe2c6c02eb544337b5c0f8: Pharaoh Kemesis has you fooled. This is clearly some ploy of his to keep us confused and divided. He is our true foe.
textByHash.28f1f30cb96e68acf3c65d2a02c5c56284f16760: You had it all in hand. I must say, this has been an interesting development.
textByHash.949f4eb7c711b3fb1f5621e9ab8b57fe701be480: No. I believe what my eyes tell me, and I am yet to see any true evidence that this is the work of an ancient goddess.
textByHash.62839b278cbcb76229bf79523f07a8440127ddee: But I will admit that there is more going on here than I first thought. Dark magic is clearly at play. This may require us to reconsider our approach.
textByHash.e4292a6bad9a1fe7d59d1639e2825a59b2c9c3d0: For now, nothing. I need to go and think on this. I suggest you seek out Maisa and see what results your meddling has caused. I'm sure I'll be in touch at some point.
textByHash.49d6e397c6d7d2f7ecdfc4595e00bcf0b0929419: I imagine Maisa will have gone to Sophanem to check on this cure. She will be waiting for you. Goodbye.
textByHash.98344e121a34d8a95a043d548096313be64d140e: My help is needed all over the place. What makes you suggest that I'm more urgently needed in Sophanem?
textByHash.80c8e0a7cb970bbea405b3f1e7c0d76a304b86e7: Maisa? You are mistaken. She's in Menaphos, and that city is locked tight to everyone, thanks in no small part to me.
textByHash.de116db40cd0a57b31a9c0958d2436762d3f81d9: Nothing you need to concern yourself with.
textByHash.e401f38e9b49f46af00c2a592e15e472f2483578: Hmm... Very well. What do you know of the political landscape here in the Kharidian Desert?
textByHash.f08ce3f637856afae7f909479d100b78864abcf5: Well unlike many other regions, the Kharidian Desert is not united under a single banner. Instead, there are multiple independent city states, along with various lawless collectives.
textByHash.c53fb9dc8372b61bb2714e24ed3ede1e3ae0d3ee: While all of the city states are currently at peace with each other, between some you'll find certain... rivalries.
textByHash.b009b58c8c76ee6fe5087780f0ce6b0e56653cdc: We were at war with the Menaphites for a long time. As Spymaster for Al Kharid, I know that no war is ever truly over.
textByHash.a5df9927c741da05325a9b089b1fcb045624305c: The Emir trusts me to protect the best interests of Al Kharid. So in answer to your original question, that's what I've been doing in Menaphos and Sophanem.
textByHash.c5445c5e020c446bf62f58ec3ba11b93715de026: Then I will send someone else down instead. Is that all?
textByHash.a8842137c36ba252effe99a988a149e1e3b4b288: That's not possible and she knows it. It would be tantamount to sticking one's head in a crocodile's mouth. If the Menaphites caught me, I'd be lynched on sight.
textByHash.2b4245c9462ad24e88d3a53334a6773d456a010f: Now, return to her and tell her that I will send a replacement for Kaleef shortly.
textByHash.06c72d5d629ab30b717970c5c31a8721fdfc3f04: If there's no way for someone to relieve Maisa, then she will have to remain there herself. This is the job she signed up for.
textByHash.31c7d334b00eff6a46cf8599942d1bb26f5e4e7f: That is your problem, not mine.
textByHash.8da988ebaf6e4201826a66683de58c6799af12cd: And you were paid for your services. What do you need from Maisa anyway?
textByHash.71450f793159e52ce10767dadb9379b3746151b4: So, you've convinced Maisa to help them on your behalf? What makes you think I want any part in this? The citizens of Sophanem are still Menaphites.
textByHash.22f0e6b18d2e6e173bc0a3a5744901910c55390b: You wish to charm me with the idea of getting my hands dirty? Well I'm perfectly happy here in Al Kharid. I did all the adventuring I ever wanted to do in my youth.
textByHash.0ad57e84ed562b19766b2c138924474a115fd9ea: That there might be, but if so, those treasures belong to the dead. We have treasures aplenty here in Al Kharid.
textByHash.70a1dfc6706ab721555c229ca997ce61ae92ef78: Hmm... Alright, you have my curiosity. Let's see where this trail takes us. Meet me outside the gates to Sophanem. I have one or two things to sort out here before I depart.
textByHash.7a5095fbe97bed324bcc0bb1ac44cbc7c5b3e29c: I have Maisa and others in place to do that already.
textByHash.e8a60e7eb861c492055ca6360d289d81c307c32a: Perhaps because there is no suitable answer. Now, I must get on with my work.
textByHash.339b403f28d9440ec645e677238e2129d4ecd635: So you've seen sense? Good. Now, I must get on with my work.
textByHash.ff9811af9e689fc19a505b31e8d4e0fd459da536: As I've already told you, I have little interest in this business of yours. Why should I help you?
textByHash.aad7b2628581c516dc0d384027756ee9194d8724: Yes, yes. I have one or two things to sort here before I depart. I'll meet you outside the gates to Sophanem.
textByHash.c1bc1aefd4d148241c42748d3460cc2e07fd3752: Ah, you're here. Do you know of a good way into the city? I'm getting too old for scaling walls.
textByHash.1656a7ab2e34f9ada075bbb92d3c2673f1d9c71e: What if I'm recognised? Do you not think they'd question the presence of Al Kharid's Spymaster?
textByHash.53ed3857eb9f531ff2fbbc1b6fe8c8f8256d7dbe: Good. Now, wait for me here. There's no point in both of us risking our necks getting seen together.
textByHash.93b84d06e1593be3a010c40d790c44ad2ce1db37: Don't worry about me. I may be getting older, but I didn't become Spymaster without getting into a fight or two.
textByHash.ecc574c3e0f1eb0bce8341c731aec1b91db484f6: Give me two hours. If I'm not back by then, come and look for me.
textByHash.a63aacef493cb911d741e8eadea8bea55127d9a3: I've no idea what's going on there. It's all on a need-to-know basis, and I don't need to know.
textByHash.fc6394f509b13eb6e42940d833cf9eb43eb20098: You can call me Osman, even though I am the man who is not Osman.
textByHash.068559d44f70975a6ae0d274040f24e2b40d9a7a: Of course I look like him. I'm in disguise. What sort of spy would look like a spy?
textByHash.bca91cf8f8bb1391b3092eb363ac0d741d348994: Right... Let's change the subject to something Osman might talk about. I don't want to blow my cover.
textByHash.7869bd1eb69440b9034085cd7cf95b3ca11c37c1: ... and whatever you do, don't risk giving yourself away when you help the citizens return.
textByHash.edc0b47664f24ecd1c57ccc8177ae61cc04e405f: Get out of here quick! Before the cave collapses!
textByHash.cda1fd173b7498cf75ffe6be98ae7f210eade4fe: I seem to recall telling you to wait for me. What are you doing down here?
textByHash.a10082e13eff475d0d96e535c0df5747968979b5: I didn't go anywhere. I decided to blend into the shadows. It's one of the many tricks I've picked up over the years. Not every problem needs a blade to solve it.
textByHash.1619072f625aaf9d11ae700f44242fe2a94b0b4f: That's not required. Once again, I had it all under control.
textByHash.66c5798e5e1ad3e43218b77e3caeee271860b368: Anyway, you'll be pleased to know that before we were interrupted, I was able to convince Maisa to remain in Menaphos. As agreed, she will help the citizens of Sophanem return home.
textByHash.bfb14fa0ce447b7c1645b3df50404c8aae3dd0c9: Before you leave, it seems that Kaleef was able to at least get one hit on that beast. I suggest you take his dagger. It's quite powerful, and he has no need for it now.
textByHash.89e9ba5f89deff1f3de25fba748ec5a5ed910df6: Well done. You did an alright job here.
textByHash.699b5c21a0c44659b896f8610d1478414d6df8b7: Our prince is captive by the Lady Keli. We just need to make the rescue. There are two things we need you to do.
textByHash.ba258aaedd2fece7abe314c41a43bc7d6c4c6c97: The prince is guarded by some stupid guards and a clever woman. The woman is our only way to get the prince out. Only she can walk freely about the area.
textByHash.4b98e96784e8b63ee19eb8ea55594c0d6ad0bbcf: I think you will need to tie her up. One coil of rope should do for that. Then, disguise the prince as her to get him out without suspicion.
textByHash.7d541c9f4da1799f04ac5c4a0f4e1626d626ba15: Only enough to fool the guards at a distance. Get a skirt like hers. Same colour, same style. We will only have a short time.
textByHash.804db57d0c9165fb971c12e7f05cb92b10badc96: Get a blonde wig, too. That is up to you to make or find. Something to colour the skin of the prince.
textByHash.346345199b3eb38382efab247f636543122de9f0: My daughter and top spy, Leela, can help you. She has sent word that she has discovered where they are keeping the prince.
textByHash.05f85c7d213b62fedc8851c53047e0a7c8f24ed8: It's near Draynor Village. She is lurking somewhere near there now.
textByHash.3c23b3201072b3ff44aea1ab6dbdfbbe44432f3d: We need the key, or we need a copy made. If you can get some soft clay then you can copy the key...
textByHash.121da9822c2c248145003eda9e749d0a7671cc18: ...If you can convince Lady Keli to show it to you for a moment. She is very boastful. It should not be too hard.
textByHash.a4d877c89ac32564c308f38c5cbef7c320b5b743: Bring the imprint to me, with a bar of bronze.
textByHash.f8ff67feaae038208c9fae8515099704dbfed962: May good luck travel with you. Don't forget to find Leela. It can't be done without her help.
textByHash.8367c8f8fc57a46ae7c163fcce7bea9e2cb5ebce: A print of the key in soft clay and a bronze bar. Then, collect the key from Leela.
textByHash.d667b70f9467af1ac1bf922a3a64bdfbcec41496: You can collect the key from Leela.
textByHash.7fa2e2f75870582968e49c7636d7767322000f02: The key you already have. Good.
textByHash.ede9b46d7219fc98714cab2c0c2c4f9e63844965: You need to make a blonde wig somehow. Leela may help.
textByHash.00dd9c868085da638e7615a0d0be59adff8af30f: The wig you have got; well done.
textByHash.8ccf3274b7d008d19e344b197df0de9096fa2b3d: You will need a skirt that looks the same as Keli's.
textByHash.5957eda34276658b35cb43779c059d3e38dc2e2e: Something to make the prince's skin appear lighter.
textByHash.709525612411d20dcf0a82c2884b56ae84ac7eec: You have the skin paint; well done. I thought you would struggle to make that.
textByHash.78e5d3e417dd572664a8eaaf23d0cf92cc87c287: A rope with which to tie Keli up.
textByHash.01fa542e00c80d68363883834a40f017f20101d5: Once you have everything, go to Leela. She must be ready to get the prince away.
textByHash.522cd6afc00532ec8cf9c29561b2f6b3b84d7885: Well done; we can make the key now.
textByHash.5992767b07de53140d8b2c59d1de8f0621780988: Pick the key up from Leela.
textByHash.296cf06dfae79c03bf2cc0fad6db080820e369c3: Foolish man! A new key will cost 15gp.
textByHash.dc7e6c08b57169d8a6af5f565a0e0a4285c70b7c: Foolish woman! A new key will cost 15gp.
textByHash.d0c98621b66266ad27df63f82db388573fe379ce: That is good. I will have another key made. You may collect it from Leela.
textByHash.f658f531649c9d13e04786285126f4cf74b191d8: Then come back to me when you do.
textByHash.b42518e051316ce6a72d3e1e784e0e4e2bb4d28e: The prince is safe and on his way home with Leela. You can pick up your payment from the chancellor.
textByHash.002a4eddbef0bd45445bc232da3f7f67391a7373: That would be an apt description. However, I find you to be an interesting choice by the Chancellor. Why has he chosen to trust you with this task over one of our own?
textByHash.a376689528137a6f1eed8cb1747d695b178d5847: That man is far too trusting at a time when we must take extra care. However, if he has made his decision, I will not question it. Still, you should know that I will be keeping a close eye on you.
textByHash.740cd80ab012fa53f6700a4cf92cfcb833f3f430: Prince Ali, heir to the Emir of Al Kharid, has been taken. My spies have already discovered where he is being held, but we need someone to make the rescue.
textByHash.06c8498c6cd3b08e5ac0e2d7bddae87bc10e9ccf: The Prince has been taken by a group of bandits led by the self-proclaimed 'Lady' Keli. They are holding him in the abandoned jail just east of Draynor Village.
textByHash.e6f640264236803924c1b5d905d51b337fa25829: According to our information, Keli is the only one able to freely move around the area. For you to get the Prince out, you will need to disguise him as her. She will of course need dealing with first.
textByHash.d7462f776ac2e8543ab73e5caf06fd59c306dbc2: And endanger the life of the Prince in the process? No. There will be no unnecessary risks. We need to do this with as little bloodshed as possible.
textByHash.7f6161c28bf7f70963ade85514fdcb8bae0230c9: She has blonde hair and wears pink clothes. My daughter, Leela, one of my spies, is currently in Draynor Village keeping an eye on the jail. I'm sure she can help you with the specifics.
textByHash.d498c7d74f19b87bd663ba0263f4cd6cd130f660: Before you go, there is one more thing. You'll need a key to get the Prince out of his cell. Keli has the only one. You could steal it, but that seems like a risk we should avoid.
textByHash.950bbed9039c0d48e8a64e66bb0119844a1a4bec: If you bring me an imprint of the key along with a bronze bar, I can show you how to make a copy. You should be able to make an imprint of the key using some soft clay.
textByHash.aff1766af6ba034e23c6dc1ae873b540c05a9148: Of course, you'll need to find a way to get Keli to show you the key without causing suspicion. I'm sure Leela can help you with that.
textByHash.50e58bb4f309b603b22a1ac468e22dd721f76d05: Indeed. Do you have any further questions before you go?
textByHash.213d796e11986db4d36b08c8cd439299c4d57a6e: No, but we have our theories.
textByHash.fc0d0da8a7c4e6efd86ed280c5e6147bb5301d8c: No. You have not yet proven yourself enough to be trusted with that information.
textByHash.c1f8e9d333640ddaa8ff88d4183959ce9c69061b: Now, do you have any further questions?
textByHash.0e235dade1a1de34d9ec7f44feaae3c601a22260: She will be somewhere near the abandoned jail that the bandits are using. It's just east of the village.
textByHash.118c29d542ad3acd6d600f631ea52d7625b93e4c: Do you have any further questions?
textByHash.9abbbbf350faa42ce2a0d264a0d4260564f442d6: You again. How are things going in Draynor?
textByHash.761441b3bd13765e69c7d90f4d15be88b2e756a7: Good. Bring me a bronze bar, and I'll get a copy made.
textByHash.a7ea17d8fd9857c58d0008ccde3b08986d9f2201: I'll use this to have a copy of the key made. I'll send it to Leela once it's ready.
textByHash.41330f3d2c227d7926c812b5eb1f47ffeb7bb1b9: Well if you need help, just talk to Leela.
textByHash.2aab9b92a0f85a9bf5cdf5a6f62246b2b6ef5205: Prince Ali is safe once more. Chancellor Hassan has your payment.
textByHash.0bc89ca3be1698c1615cf87fc802c4f7339debf2: I thank you for your hospitality.
textByHash.fd7b96aa87b3cbc4da0c636ecdb2439457539284: Thank you very much . If you get some more sq'irks be sure to come back.
textByHash.81d89c3d2a7b186db51a3bc73e40d8687512cb78: There is a sorceress near the south-eastern edge of Al Kharid who grows them. She used to be friends with Osman, but they fell out.
textByHash.c9f740650668032e8d9fee0aba15fb2002ba1343: I don't know. It's impossible to get any information out of Osman. Anyway, now she won't give us any more fruit.
textByHash.c918926fa22286542637307a69c2071b00a7ef41: I doubt it will be that easy. She is not renowned for her generosity and is very secretive about her garden's location.
textByHash.103cceb174f6655b5a60be24fd816e88c3d8c76d: Her garden has remained hidden from even Osman - the chief spy of Al Kharid. I believe her garden must be hidden by magical means.
textByHash.7abec2902af38af485e0d537d6e01c7aec32732d: I'll reward you for as many as you can get your hands on, but could you please squeeze the fruit into a grass first?
textByHash.babbe22bc846e90129c78a56539e83044ad52a03: What would you like to know?
textByHash.41884e6ff2e77f0f4ed034d5e0cafd60f732d0ba: Osman and the Sorceress had a falling out. Now she won't give any of us spies fruit from her garden.
textByHash.1b37d1a55a3e191c7dec471d78a6caead86b32d2: Use a pestle and mortar to squeeze the sq'irks. Make sure you have an empty glass with you to collect the juice.
textByHash.3e0fb2d1145165f6de4f4b73809d0050e34a7f06: Of course there is. I am a generous man. I'll teach you the art of Thieving for your troubles.
textByHash.653f7f8d98833c498dc53b43c9469803788c85a2: That depends on the quantity and ripeness of the sq'irks you put into the juice.
textByHash.8ef31c49c4d8943081cf9a520338f6dc8b15e610: Ah it's sweet, sweet nectar for a thief or spy; it makes light fingers lighter, fleet feet flightier and comes in four different colours for those who are easily amused.
textByHash.a22797f4ea54413bc9197a6009cc57cd78004d05: It only holds power over those with poor self-control, something which I have an abundance of.
textByHash.787327487e8e569bad2ae7c09a0f28ea87ae8032: I've no idea what's going on there. It's all on a need-to- know basis, and I don't need to know.
textByHash.67b105a7720cd4e2d37a445074b3d8674278ce00: Well done. I have new information to share with you.
textByHash.dcaf7f147677aa2dcd137f4805dbf775d8b37720: Hello. I am Osman. What can I assist you with?
textByHash.15f9d1928ba424d591786c84e0647c0c97a5be5f: I work for Al Kharid's Emir. That is all you need to know.
textByHash.324dfd9ee536b82c4052353e55c01f69c40e7064: That bothers me not. The secrets of Al Kharid protect themselves.
textByHash.43b5f915479f50509e24037ca55800d99744c7c7: Well done. A great rescue. I will remember you if I have anything dangerous to do.
textByHash.122860a5b7d96b6b9689521d7024e8eb13e0fd0e: Yes, quite the interesting set of events. I suspect I won't be heading back down there any time soon.
textByHash.07b1f66de963eb6de33092784498d62e62227a29: That reminds me, I have Kaleef's dagger here for you. I imagine you'll find it rather useful.
textByHash.a848bbe7b26aa0fee692adaeb770af89a46c73e3: Of course. They're a standard piece of equipment for us. The enchantments upon them make them very useful against both scarabs and kalphites. If you want, I can sell you another for 20,000 coins?
textByHash.70fcf07eedd6cb8c0a4da65a64329e939b210310: Well you know where I am if you reconsider.
textByHash.ee28fc7fc3db5f22ff68176eb86100dd4377bc62: I think you already know the answer to that.
textByHash.72603861f4ed80f3f118e984db0ea51e28c17f58: There is a sorceress near the south-eastern edge of Al Kharid who grows them. Once upon a time, we considered each other friends.
textByHash.269f3f1494317aa6dfc18794ed3836052c57e409: We fell out, and now she won't give me any more fruit.
textByHash.d8db0be9fd24d0f3dc6923e34e36f8b8d71881ec: Her garden has remained hidden even to me - the chief spy of Al Kharid. I believe her garden must be hidden by magical means.
textByHash.c6b1b8eb308c37dd2df9de57eb457391fedf5f5f: I'll reward you for as many as you can get your hands on, but could you please squeeze the fruit into a glass first?
textByHash.110f4afa3ae43d68f658eb7ba452b6e8821d6708: I may have mentioned that I had a falling out with the Sorceress. Well, unsurprisingly, she refuses to give me any more of her garden's produce.
